rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SHORTALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE UDELHOVEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant Marcal Associates, Ltd.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages is off calendar. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration and Mr. Ay¿s declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Mono-kote fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability and false representation causes of action and claim for punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM HUNT VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4, Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to issue 1 (strict liability) and denied as to issue 2 (premise owner/contractor liability). As to issue 1, plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his strict liability cause of action. As to issue 2, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff cannot prevail on his premises owner/contractor liability cause of action. Plaintiff alleges in this cause of action that defendant¿s employees performed affirmative work that exposed him to asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE WRIGHT VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5, Defendant Mission Stucco Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s causes of action for negligence and strict liability are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration, defendant¿s responses to General Order No. 129 interrogatories and the deposition testimony of Mr. Hernandez create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Mission exterior color coat stucco) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and the deposition testimony of Mr. Hernandez are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY, PATRICIA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEIR, AND AS et al VS. KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, FEBRUARY 19, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 6, Defendant Dynalectric Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Casey. Even accepting plaintiffs¿ argument that the duty was created pursuant to the 1955 General Industry Safety Orders (GISOs), plaintiffs failed to submit evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known that the dust allegedly disturbed by defendant¿s employees in Mr. Casey¿s presence was hazardous (i.e. asbestos) and exceeded the dust count acceptable under the 1955 GISOs. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declarations regarding whether the dust allegedly disturbed by defendant¿s employees exceeded the hazardous levels are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
